subject: ELA
title: Reading: Space Exploration
description: Informational text comprehension and author's craft.
passage:
The New Space Race

Space exploration has entered an exciting new era. While governments like NASA still lead major missions, private companies are now playing a huge role. Companies like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Virgin Galactic are building rockets and spacecraft with the goal of making space travel more affordable and accessible.

SpaceX, founded by Elon Musk in 2002, made history in 2020 when it became the first private company to send astronauts to the International Space Station (ISS). The company is also developing the Starship rocket, which it hopes will one day carry humans to Mars.

But why go to space at all? Scientists argue that space exploration benefits life on Earth. Many technologies we use every day — including GPS, weather satellites, and even scratch-resistant eyeglass lenses — were developed because of the space program.

Critics worry about the cost and environmental impact of rocket launches. Rockets burn large amounts of fuel and release emissions. However, engineers are working on greener alternatives, including rockets powered by liquid hydrogen.

Whatever the challenges, human curiosity about what lies beyond our planet continues to drive the adventure of space exploration.

options: It was first private company to send astronauts to ISS
questions.answer: It was first private company to send astronauts to ISS
questions.explanation: SpaceX became the first private company to send astronauts to the ISS in 2020.

questions.q: Which everyday technology came from the space program?
questions.explanation: GPS is listed as a technology developed because of the space program.

questions.q: What is the AUTHOR'S PURPOSE in writing this passage?
options: To persuade readers to become astronauts
To inform about modern space exploration
To argue that space is too expensive
questions.answer: To inform about modern space exploration
questions.explanation: The passage presents multiple facts and perspectives — its purpose is to inform.

questions.q: What fuel are engineers developing as a greener alternative? (two words)
questions.explanation: The passage states 'rockets powered by liquid hydrogen.'

questions.q: What does ACCESSIBLE mean in the first paragraph?
questions.explanation: Making travel 'more affordable and accessible' = reachable by more people.

questions.q: Which best describes the STRUCTURE of this passage?
questions.explanation: The passage presents history, benefits, and criticisms — multiple perspectives.

questions.q: Which statement is supported by EVIDENCE in the passage?
options: SpaceX plans to go to Mars
questions.answer: SpaceX plans to go to Mars
questions.explanation: The passage mentions Starship is being developed to carry humans to Mars.

questions.q: Name ONE criticism of space exploration mentioned in the passage. (2-3 words)
questions.explanation: Critics worry about cost and environmental impact of rocket launches.

questions.q: What would be the BEST summary of this passage?
options: SpaceX is the only space company that matters.
Space exploration is dangerous and should stop.
Space exploration has entered a new era with private companies playing a key role, bringing both benefits and challenges.
Rockets are bad for the environment.
questions.answer: Space exploration has entered a new era with private companies playing a key role, bringing both benefits and challenges.
questions.explanation: This covers all main ideas: new era, private companies, benefits, and challenges.

questions.q: The author uses the phrase 'human curiosity' in the final paragraph. What does this suggest?
options: Our desire to explore is a natural and powerful force
questions.answer: Our desire to explore is a natural and powerful force
questions.explanation: The author ends by saying curiosity 'drives the adventure' — suggesting it is fundamental to human nature.
